titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 572

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=886 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:12:05.441207+00 | translation moa=887 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:12:05.452625+00

p.cl: . Dauid dei gratia Rex Scot’ . Omnibus Sanctę Ecclsię fidelibus presentibus et futuris salutem .

Sciatis me dedisse . et concessisse .
Ecclesię Sanctę Marię et Sancti Cuthberti de Coldingham . et Monachis ibidem deseruientibus . Ederham . et Nesebitam in perpetuam
elemosinam . sicut Cospatricus frater Dolfini eas tenuit die qua fuit uiuus et mortuus cum suis rectis diuisis . ita liberas .
et quietas sicut alias terras tenent que ad Coldingham pertinent . et sicut Carta eorum testatur . in ęcclesiis . et aquis . et pratis .
et pascuis . et molendinis . et Omnibus aliis locis .

Presentibus testibus . Hereberto Abbate de Rochesburc . Daniele priore de
Geddewrda . Aschelin archidiacono de Glasgv . Willelmo filio Dunecan . Dunecano Comite . Hugone de Moreuilla . Ger
uasio Ridell’ . Alano filio Waldeof . et Gospatrico fratre suo . Willelmo de Sumeruilla . Willelmo de Graham .

Apud Rochesburc .
xvii . kl . Septembris . Anno . M . C . xxx . ix .
David, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all Holy Church's faithful, present and to come, greeting.

Know me to have given and conceded to the church of St Mary and St Cuthbert at Coldingham, and to the monks serving there, Edrom and Nisbet in perpetual alms, just as Cospatric brother of Dolphin held them on the day he was alive and dead, with their correct boundaries, as free and quit as they hold other lands which belong to Coldingham, and just as their charter bears witness, in churches, waters, meadows, pastures, mills, and all other places.

Present as witnesses: Herbert the abbot of Roxburgh, Daniel the prior of Jedburgh, Ascelin the archdeacon of Glasgow, William son of Duncan, Duncan the earl, Hugh de Morville, Gervase Ridel, Alan son of Waldeve, and Cospatric his own brother, William de Somerville, William of Graham.
At Roxburgh on the 17th day before the kalends of September [16th August] in the year 1139.
